modules.title: Module 1 · Angles in a Circle
modules.html:
1. Angle at centre is twice the angle at circumference subtended by the same arc.
  2. Angles in the same segment are equal.
  3. Angle in a semicircle is a right angle ($90°$).

examples.q: The angle subtended by an arc at the centre is $84°$. Find the angle at the circumference.

modules.title: Module 2 · Cyclic Quadrilaterals
modules.html: A quadrilateral whose vertices lie on a circle. Its opposite angles are supplementary: $A + C = 180°$ and $B + D = 180°$. The exterior angle equals the interior opposite angle.

examples.q: In a cyclic quadrilateral $ABCD$, $\angle A = 110°$. Find $\angle C$.

modules.title: Module 3 · Tangent Properties
modules.html:
• A tangent to a circle is perpendicular to the radius at the point of contact.
  • Two tangents from an external point are equal in length.
  • Alternate segment theorem: the angle between a tangent and a chord equals the angle in the alternate segment.

examples.q: Two tangents $PA$ and $PB$ are drawn from external point $P$. If $PA = 8$ cm, find $PB$.

quiz.q: An arc subtends an angle of $110°$ at the centre. What angle does it subtend at the circumference?
quiz.solution: Angle at centre is twice the angle at the circumference: $110° \div 2 = 55°$.

quiz.q: An arc subtends an angle of $140°$ at the centre. What angle does it subtend at the circumference?
quiz.solution: Angle at centre is twice the angle at the circumference: $140° \div 2 = 70°$.

quiz.q: In cyclic quadrilateral $ABCD$, $\angle A = 125°$. Find $\angle C$.
quiz.solution: Opposite angles of a cyclic quadrilateral sum to $180°$: $\angle C = 180° - 125° = 55°$.

quiz.q: In cyclic quadrilateral $ABCD$, $\angle A = 88°$. Find $\angle C$.

quiz.q: The angle in a semicircle is:
quiz.solution: The angle subtended by a diameter at the circumference is a right angle.

quiz.q: Two tangents $PA$ and $PB$ are drawn from an external point $P$ to a circle. If $PA = 7$ cm, find $PB$.
quiz.solution: Tangents drawn to a circle from an external point are equal in length.

quiz.q: What is the angle between a tangent and the radius drawn to the point of contact?
quiz.solution: A tangent to a circle is perpendicular to the radius at the point of tangency.
